last_excerpt: and the ring stain was more specific and more honest than anything in the article. The receptionist's desk had one of those name plaques, black plastic with white letters, and the first letter of her last name had fallen out so it read _OWELL, and nobody had fixed it, which means either she didn't care or she'd stopped seeing it. Both of those feel the same from the outside. There's a kind of paint on gymnasium floors that's so thick it has its own texture, almost rubbery, and when you drag your shoe across it the squeak isn't friction exactly, it's the paint deforming and snapping back, and every basketball game played on that floor is played on top of eleven other colors of paint going back decades. The oldest layer is probably lead-based. I don't know what color they painted gym floors in the 1950s but I'd guess green.
last_concepts: the sound of a spoon hitting the bottom of an empty bowl
how a water wheel converts flow into rotation
how a pendulum's period depends only on length, not weight
the last ice cube melting in a glass
